question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open thousands of concurrent gRPC streams via subscribeTopic() with no authentication barrier, exhausting server-side thread/connection resources and causing a network partition where legitimate nodes can no longer reach the mirror node service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with limit=0 (no limit) and startTime in the distant past, causing subscribeTopic() to emit an unbounded Flux that never terminates, holding open a reactive subscription indefinitely and partitioning the server from processing new connections?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a ConsensusTopicQuery with no topicID set, causing toFilter() to build a TopicMessageFilter with a null topicId that bypasses EntityId validation and triggers an unhandled NullPointerException deep in the reactive chain, disrupting the stream processing pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: convertTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a Timestamp with seconds=9223372035 (boundary value) in consensusStartTime, causing convertTimestamp() to return Long.MAX_VALUE as startTime, which when passed to TopicMessageFilter causes the filter's isValidStartTime() check to fail with a ConstraintViolationException that floods error logs and degrades server throughput?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: convertTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user set consensusStartTime.seconds to exactly 9223372034 (just below the boundary) so convertTimestamp() calls DomainUtils.timestampInNanosMax() with a value that overflows long nanosecond arithmetic, producing a negative startTime that bypasses the isValidStartTime() constraint and creates a subscription anchored to an invalid time window?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user rapidly open and cancel gRPC streams (triggering setOnCancelHandler -> disposable::dispose repeatedly) to cause a high-frequency dispose/subscribe cycle on the reactive pipeline, exhausting bounded elastic scheduler threads in TopicMessageServiceImpl and partitioning the service from the database?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with consensusStartTime set to the current time and no endTime, causing subscribeTopic() to create a live subscription that holds a database polling connection open indefinitely, and by repeating this across many connections, exhaust the database connection pool and partition the mirror node from its data source?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with consensusStartTime equal to consensusEndTime, causing toFilter() to build a filter where isValidEndTime() returns false (endTime not > startTime), triggering a ConstraintViolationException that is mapped by onErrorMap(ProtoUtil::toStatusRuntimeException) and returned as INVALID_ARGUMENT, but if done at high rate, saturates the error-handling path and degrades service availability?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic that contains messages with a null runningHashVersion, causing toResponse() to call Objects.requireNonNullElse(t.getRunningHashVersion(), DEFAULT_RUNNING_HASH_VERSION) on every message, and if the topic has millions of messages, cause CPU saturation during the mapping phase that partitions the gRPC server from responding to other clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseTransactionID()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic where stored messages contain malformed transactionIdBytes, causing parseTransactionID() to throw InvalidProtocolBufferException repeatedly, and the resulting log.error() calls at high volume saturate the logging subsystem and cause I/O blocking that partitions the gRPC service from its network interfaces?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription with a very large limit value (Long.MAX_VALUE) and a startTime far in the past, causing the historical Flux from topicMessageRetriever.retrieve() to emit an enormous number of messages that overwhelm the reactive backpressure mechanism and cause an overflow exception, which ProtoUtil maps to DEADLINE_EXCEEDED, but the resource consumption before that point partitions the server?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of per-client rate limiting in subscribeTopic() to open N simultaneous subscriptions to the same high-traffic topic, causing the subscriberCount AtomicLong to increment to N and the server to emit N copies of every message, multiplying outbound bandwidth N-fold and causing a network partition for other clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with a topicID whose shard/realm/num combination encodes to an EntityId that maps to a non-existent entity, causing toFilter() to build a valid filter that passes to topicExists() which then emits EntityNotFoundException, and by flooding with such requests, exhaust the EntityRepository query thread pool and partition the service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with consensusEndTime set to a timestamp just 1 nanosecond in the future, causing subscribeTopic() to create a subscription that immediately triggers the pastEndTime() Flux, but the 1-second safetyCheck Mono.delay() still fires and issues a missingMessages() database query, creating a race condition that leaves dangling database connections?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Mono.fromCallable(() -> toFilter(request)) pattern by sending a malformed ConsensusTopicQuery that causes toFilter() to throw an unchecked exception not caught by onErrorMap(ProtoUtil::toStatusRuntimeException), resulting in an unhandled error that terminates the reactive chain without calling responseObserver::onCompleted, leaking the gRPC stream and its associated resources?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: convertTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user send a Timestamp with seconds=Long.MAX_VALUE/1000000000 and nanos=999999999 to convertTimestamp(), causing DomainUtils.timestampInNanosMax() to produce a value that overflows when stored in the TopicMessageFilter, leading to a startTime that is interpreted as a negative nanosecond value and causing the database query to scan the entire message table?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user send repeated ConsensusTopicQuery requests with identical parameters to subscribeTopic(), causing the TopicMessageServiceImpl to create duplicate TopicContext instances that each independently poll the database via the safetyCheck Mono.delay(), multiplying database load and causing a network partition between the mirror node and its PostgreSQL backend?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of connection-level authentication in subscribeTopic() to open a subscription, then deliberately slow-read responses (TCP receive window manipulation), causing the reactive Flux to buffer indefinitely in memory, eventually triggering an OutOfMemoryError that crashes the JVM and partitions all network connections?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic with messages containing extremely large message payloads (t.getMessage()), causing toResponse() to call ProtoUtil.toByteString() with large byte arrays via UnsafeByteOperations.unsafeWrap(), and by subscribing to many such topics simultaneously, exhaust heap memory and cause a network partition through JVM crash?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with limit=1 and startTime far in the past, causing subscribeTopic() to retrieve one historical message and complete, but by sending this at extremely high frequency (thousands per second), exhaust the gRPC server's request processing threads and partition the service from legitimate subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the setOnCancelHandler(disposable::dispose) registration to send a cancel signal immediately after subscribing, before the Mono.fromCallable(() -> toFilter(request)) completes, causing a race condition where dispose() is called on a not-yet-initialized Disposable, resulting in a NullPointerException that propagates through the reactive chain and corrupts the stream state?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with consensusStartTime.nanos set to a value greater than 999999999 (invalid nanoseconds), causing convertTimestamp() to pass an invalid Timestamp to DomainUtils.timestampInNanosMax() which may produce an arithmetic overflow, resulting in a startTime that causes the database query to return unexpected results or scan unbounded data?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open a long-lived subscription to a high-volume topic and then introduce artificial TCP latency (via network manipulation on their end), causing the gRPC server's write buffer to fill up, which triggers backpressure in the reactive Flux, stalling the entire subscription pipeline and blocking the bounded elastic scheduler threads shared with other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that subscribeTopic() uses Mono.fromCallable() (which runs on the calling thread) to send a ConsensusTopicQuery that causes toFilter() to perform a blocking operation, tying up the gRPC I/O thread and preventing the server from accepting new connections, effectively causing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic where messages have chunkNum set but chunkTotal is 0 or negative, causing toResponse() to build a ConsensusMessageChunkInfo with invalid total, and if the downstream client processes this in a loop expecting total > 0, cause an infinite loop or division-by-zero on the client side that keeps the gRPC stream open indefinitely?
